title: CI: cherry-pick command is not working
body:
### Issue Description

The Cherry Pick to Release Branch workflow (.github/workflows/cherry-pick.yml) fails on the "Create cherry-pick PR" step after the cherry-pick itself succeeds

### Steps to reproduce the issue

  1. On a merged PR in containers/podman, a maintainer posts a comment: /cherry-pick <release-branch> (e.g. /cherry-pick v5.8).
  2. The Cherry Pick to Release Branch workflow runs. The "Cherry-pick to release branch" step succeeds and pushes cherry-pick-<PR>-to-<branch>.
  3. The next step, "Create cherry-pick PR", fails with exit code 4.


### Describe the results you received
https://github.com/containers/podman/actions/runs/24150148060/job/70474588085
Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

"Create cherry-pick PR" successfully opens a backport PR titled [<branch>] <original PR title> with a body referencing the source PR, requester, and original merge SHA.

comments.body: I did point this out when we discussed removing the openshift merge bots and the like.  So it sounds like we never added a replacement ?
@timcoding1988 you might put this as a priority or see if someone else can do it (assuming they have time), because this is about to get really important.

> I did point this out when we discussed removing the openshift merge bots and the like. So it sounds like we never added a replacement ?

There's [some thing](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/.github/workflows/cherry-pick.yml) but it needs fixing.
Another issue: the cherry pick branches are on containers/podman. I feel like they should live in a fork instead of upstream.

> Another issue: the cherry pick branches are on containers/podman. I feel like they should live in a fork instead of upstream.

we'll still need cherrypick support for the release branches though, won't we?
Wait, nevermind, renovate does the same thing, but we should make sure to delete the branches once the PR is merged.

> Wait, nevermind, renovate does the same thing, but we should make sure to delete the branches once the PR is merged.

sorry, I see what you mean @ashley-cui
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
